Title: A cell wall–localized cytokinin/purine riboside nucleosidase is involved in apoplastic cytokinin metabolism in Oryza sativa
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: trans-zeatin; cis-zeatin and their riboside derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant growth and development regulation; nitrogen-responsive shoot signaling; grain yield/panicle development
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: LOG-independent cytokinin activation pathway; apoplastic cytokinin metabolism; purine nucleoside metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: inosine/uridine-preferring nucleoside hydrolase domain-containing protein; two ribosidase domains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deribosylates cytokinin ribosides and related purine nucleosides, converting tZR to tZ, cZR to cZ, and hydrolyzing tZROG and cZROG; also hydrolyzes adenosine, inosine, and guanosine to corresponding bases.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: tZR; cZR; tZROG; cZROG; adenosine; inosine; guanosine
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream activation step; terminal precursor-activating step in LOG-independent pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms active cytokinin nucleobases from riboside precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for apoplastic cytokinin riboside nucleosidase activity under tested conditions; almost fully responsible for tZR-hydrolyzing activity in shoot extracts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function increases cytokinin ribosides (tZR, cZR, cZROG, tZROG) in shoots and apoplastic fluid, reduces cZOG, attenuates tZR-responsive cytokinin signaling, and decreases panicle weight and grain number-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL fine mapping; Tos17 knockout mutants; RT-PCR; recombinant protein enzyme assay; crude extract activity assay; apoplastic cytokinin profiling; GFP localization; cytokinin treatment response; nitrogen resupply assay; field phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in panicle branches, flag leaf blades and sheaths, and lower leaf blades; higher in shoots than roots in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in panicle branches, flag leaf blades and sheaths, and lower leaf blades; higher in shoots than roots in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: tZ/tZR treatment of detached leaves; nitrogen starvation followed by NH4NO3 resupply; field-grown conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces panicle weight, rachis length, grain number per panicle, and secondary branch number; possible confounding effects through altered purine metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S promoter used for transient CPN1:GFP localization in onion epidermal cells
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phytohormone profiling/metabolomics of CSSL population; QTL mapping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate extracellular cytokinin activation and nitrogen-responsive cytokinin signaling in leaves and panicles; engineering should consider tradeoffs in panicle architecture and dual effects on purine metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QTL mapping of cytokinin riboside traits identified Os05g0406100/CPN1 as the causal locus affecting cZROG accumulation. 2. Recombinant CPN1 directly deribosylates cytokinin ribosides including tZR and cZR, establishing it as a cytokinin/purine riboside nucleosidase. 3. cpn1 mutants accumulate cytokinin ribosides in shoots and leaf apoplast, lose detectable tZR-hydrolyzing activity, show reduced tZR-responsive OsRR expression, and display impaired panicle development in the field.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein start site was revised by 5' RACE, yielding an 889-aa full-length protein. Habataki allele showed weaker expression than Sasanishiki in the mapped region. The paper notes CPN1 likely has dual roles in extracellular cytokinin and purine metabolism. The manuscript contains a likely typo in one place listing Os06g0406100, but mapped gene throughout Results is Os05g0406100.
